seoTitle: How to Change Microsoft Teams Password Easily (2026)
metaDescription: Learn how to update your Microsoft Teams password through account settings, self-service reset tools, and multi-factor verification.
intro: Since Teams credentials are tied to your broader Microsoft 365 account, updating your password depends on knowing whether you're on a personal or managed work profile. This guide covers navigating to the security info panel, changing your password with current credential verification, using self-service reset if you're locked out, and when to involve your IT administrator. It's a straightforward walkthrough for anyone responding to an expired password or a security alert.

steps.heading: Check your account type
steps.body: Click your profile avatar and select View Account to confirm whether you're on a personal Microsoft account or an enterprise managed work directory.

steps.heading: Go to security settings
steps.body: Redirect to the Microsoft My Sign-Ins or Security info web console dashboard.

steps.heading: Change your password
steps.body: Locate the Password section, click Change Password, enter your current password, then type and confirm a new strong password.

steps.heading: Submit the change
steps.body: Click submit to authorize the update and reset your active authentication tokens across devices.

steps.heading: Reset a forgotten password
steps.body: If you're locked out, click Forgot my password on the sign-in screen and verify your identity using multi-factor authentication codes.

faq.question: Why can't I change my Teams password directly inside the app?
faq.answer: Teams passwords are managed through the broader Microsoft 365 identity system, so changes happen through the account security portal, not inside the Teams app itself.

faq.question: What if my organization blocks self-service password resets?
faq.answer: If policy blocks the reset, you'll need to route the request directly to your IT directory infrastructure administrators.

faq.question: Will changing my password log me out of other devices?
faq.answer: Yes, submitting a password change resets active authentication tokens, which typically requires you to sign back in on all your devices.

faq.question: What do I need to reset a forgotten password?
faq.answer: You'll typically need access to a recovery email or an authenticator app to complete multi-factor verification during the reset process.
